question: Is the image rendered on a white background with a horizontal canvas?
Does the image depict a single cube in isometric projection (no perspective convergence)?
Are the cube's three edge groups oriented at approximately ±30° to the horizontal and vertical directions?
Are there exactly 8 vertices labeled A, B, C, D (top face, clockwise) and E, F, G, H (bottom face, corresponding directly below)?
Are the vertices marked with small solid black dots?
Is the vertex connectivity correct: Top A-B-C-D, Bottom E-F-G-H, and Vertical edges A-E, B-F, C-G, D-H?
Are visible cube edges rendered with thick black solid lines?
Are hidden cube edges rendered with thin gray dashed lines?
Are there exactly 6 midpoints labeled P1 through P6 marked with blue hollow circles?
Are the midpoints correctly located on edges in this sequence: P1 on AB, P2 on BC, P3 on CG, P4 on GH, P5 on HE, and P6 on EA?
Are points P1-P6 connected by thick blue dashed lines to form a closed hexagonal section?
Is the hexagon interior filled with a very light blue semi-transparent color (no gradient)?
Are thin gray auxiliary lines drawn from each point Pi to both endpoints of its respective edge?
Is there a blue normal vector arrow labeled 'n' perpendicular to the section plane?
Is there a label 'PLANE π' with a thin gray leader line pointing to the section?
Is there a coordinate triad labeled x, y, z with thin black arrows located in the bottom left corner?
Does the diagram strictly use only black, gray, and blue colors (excluding white background)?
Are shadows, gradients (other than the specified transparency), and perspective distortion completely absent?
Are all text labels (A-H, P1-P6, n, PLANE π, x-y-z) clearly readable and free of garbled characters?
